title: Light an LED with a resistor
description: The classic first circuit: a battery, a current-limiting resistor, and an LED. Learn why the resistor is there and watch current flow once you hit Simulate.
author: Breadboard Sim Team

steps.instructions: Start with power. Select Battery in the palette, click a hole on the top + rail, then click the hole directly below it on the top − rail. This is our 5V source.
An LED can only handle a small amount of current before it burns out, so we limit it with a resistor. Select Resistor, click a hole on the + rail, then click a hole in the terminal strip below it.
Now the LED itself. Select LED, click first for the anode (+, current flows in) in the same column as the resistor's free end, then click for the cathode (−) one column over.
Complete the loop back to the battery. Select Wire and connect the LED's cathode column to the − rail.

Hit Simulate in the top bar. The LED should light up and you'll see an animated dot showing current direction. With 5V and a 220Ω resistor, Ohm's law says roughly (5V − ~2V LED drop) / 220Ω ≈ 14mA is flowing — click the resistor or LED in the Inspector to check the real reading.
